notes:
kia.com/us/en/<model> consumer site. Hybrid and PHEV variants of mainstream models live on the parent model's page (e.g., sportage-hybrid points to /sportage-hybrid). Phase B (2026-05): carnival and carnival-hybrid migrated from /carnival and /carnival-hybrid to /carnival-mpv and /carnival-mpv-hybrid (404 on old paths). Phase 3 session 6 (2026-05-14): kia.com hosts hybrid imagery under CDN folders named `<model>-hev` (sorento-hev, sportage-hev, niro-hev, carnival-hev) and PHEV under `<model>-phev` — these slug_variants additions close the match gap. K4 hatchback uses the URL token `k4hb`. Removed the bare `niro` variant from niro-hybrid: it collided with niro-ev imagery (the URL `kia-niro-ev-mmm` would also satisfy a bare `niro` match).

Session 7 Phase A3 (2026-05-14): added angle_url_patterns. Kia's CDN URLs and alt text rarely carry English angle words; hero variants 1920/1440/1024 are empty-alt and only the small 375 variant has descriptive alt text. Standard ANGLE_PATTERNS catches the front/side cases via existing `front[-_ ]?view` (space-tolerant) and `(?:^|[-_/ ])side(?:...)` (also space-tolerant) rules. Only one brand pattern added:
  - front_three_quarter ← `\bgallery[-_]?ext\d+\b` (the kia in-page-gallery exterior-numbered files — verified front-3/4 on seltos ext1, k5 ext2, sorento ext3, niro-hev ext1). The `-ext` infix is the precision lever: without it `gallery-N` numbering picked up interior shots (sportage-hybrid `gallery-6` was a cabin shot) and rear shots (k4-hatchback `gallery-01`). Risk: low — `-ext` guarantees an exterior shot; kia's gallery convention puts a front-3/4 hero first.
No confident patterns for rear_three_quarter (the candidate `\bthree[-_ ]?quarter[-_ ]?back\b` alt-text pattern was tested and abandoned — kia's niro-hybrid 375 global-hero file alt says "three-quarter back view" but the actual image is a front 3/4 view; alt is mislabeled on the source CDN, so the pattern would mis-place that image), side_profile (standard `side` rule already catches kia's "driver's-side view" alts), or interior_dashboard (zero interior URLs surfaced on the 7-page static fetch). Hero-path patterns (`/global-hero/`, `mep-hero-v\d`) were considered and rejected: kia chose different angles for different model heroes, so a single hero regex would create false positives. See reports/kia_angle_patterns_session7.md.
